items.title: Install correctly
items.why: Guarantees the package lands in the kernel's environment.
items.example: `%pip install langchain==0.2.0` — installed into whichever Python the kernel is using; the next `import langchain` works without a restart.

items.title: Measure properly
items.example: Comparing two tokenisers? `%timeit enc_a.encode(doc)` vs `%timeit enc_b.encode(doc)` — both give µs-precision stable numbers you can actually compare.

items.title: Learn the modes
items.avoid: Reaching for the mouse for every action.
items.prefer: Esc/Enter to switch modes; Shift+Enter, a/b/dd to manage cells.
items.example: `Esc, A` (insert above), `Esc, B` (insert below), `Esc, DD` (delete), `Esc, M` (to markdown). Two seconds vs ten with the mouse.

items.title: Keep magics in notebooks
items.avoid: Copying magic-laden cells into reusable .py code.
items.prefer: Refactor logic into plain-Python functions for scripts.
items.example: Replace `%timeit f()` in a script with `from time import perf_counter; t=perf_counter(); f(); print(perf_counter()-t)` — same intent, valid Python.
